preamble: Monophthongization is a sound change where a diphthong simplifies into a single, stable vowel sound. This process often reduces articulatory effort and can be found in the historical development of languages like French and various dialects of English.

examples.note: Vedic diphthongs became monophthongs in Classical Sanskrit

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
